purpose: Quantify per-round UX-quality improvement. Stop when round-over-round delta < 1% — Luba's convergence target.
measurement_tool: scripts/ux-quality-score.sh v2 (RUN-137, supersedes v1 from RUN-135)
measurement_method: v2 = weighted offence count, USER-VISIBLE lines only. Strips JSDoc (`/** */`), line comments (`//`), JSX comments (`{/* */}`). ENGINE_INTERNAL uses dual-count: total minus `classification="ENGINE_INTERNAL"` prop usages (code identifier rendered as the user-facing 'Platform-only' pill after RUN-122). v1 was replaced because it counted BFA/arbiter references inside JSDoc audit-trail headers as offences, inflating scores by ~50 pts and distorting deltas. Measured by checking out the historical tree and running the v2 script against it, so scores are reproducible from history.

convergence_target.rationale: When each new round produces less than 1% quality improvement, the sweep has saturated and remaining defects are either per-cell fixes (not bulk patterns) or architecturally deferred (require product decisions).
convergence_target.status_after_round_4: CONVERGED — R4 delta = 0% meets the < 1% criterion. Trajectory: R0=349 → R1=235 → R2=106 → R3=51 → R4=51. Total improvement from R0: -298 pts / -85.4% under v2 scoring.

rounds.label: pre-refresh baseline (RUN-86 last commit before Round 1)
rounds.scope: Round 1 (RUN-87..115b, 28 commits) produces the first measured improvement against this baseline.
rounds.note: v1 scored 402. v2 restates at 349 because v1 counted JSDoc and comment references plus 14 code-identifier `classification="ENGINE_INTERNAL"` prop usages as offences. v2 filters them out, giving a cleaner floor. Coverage matrix didn't exist at this commit; the 17 NOT_YET_EXAMINED figure reflects the current HEAD matrix (frozen-matrix comparison regime) so all rounds share the same coverage-gap denominator.

rounds.label: end of Round 1 — design-skills refresh + per-flow signature + hero-metric sweep
rounds.notes: Round 1 focused on hero-metric elimination (58 → 0), emoji-icons, and FLOW-06/07/08/10/12/17/20/22 signature/composition upgrades. Plain-language acronym sweep had not started yet — that's why BFA/DNA/arbiter counts are identical to R0. Under v2 scoring (comment-filtered) the improvement is 32.7% vs v1's 28.9% because the 53-pt comment+prop overhead isn't double-counted.

rounds.label: end of Round 2 — RTL baseline + module shell + plain-language sweep + coverage matrix + CFI-11 governance sync
rounds.notes: Round 2 killed BFA 10→0, DNA 13→0, arbiter 11→1, FREEDOM 2→0, DataProcessResult 1→0 user-visible. Only 1 arbiter leak remained at R2-end (fixed in RUN-137 — see HEAD row). ENGINE_INTERNAL visible leaks already 0 at R0 because the only hits are code-identifier prop usages rendered as 'Platform-only' pill. T-numbers 18→12 (6 JSDoc comments gone); CF-numbers 18→16 (kept CF-842 rule-id cross-references for platform-admin). Round 2 improvement is DOUBLE Round 1 under v2 scoring because R2 did the acronym sweep that R1 hadn't touched.

rounds.label: Round 3 — targeted cleanup of remaining visible leaks + RTL logical-property sweep + coverage matrix population
rounds.target_met: YES — 51.9% actual vs 5%+ required, 5x the stretch target (10%)
closed_targets: BFA user-visible leaks 10→0 (retained under v2 after RUN-137 AdsPlatformPage fix)
Physical-direction classes text-left/right on admin pages 29→0 (RUN-139 logical-property sweep)
T-number visible leaks 12→5 (RUN-140 fixed body-copy + stub data; 5 remaining are RUN-131 intentional taskTypeId '(T617)' suffixes on DesignSystemGovernance platform-admin compat reports)
9 NOT_YET_EXAMINED flows examined (RUN-141 + RUN-142): 6 pass clean, 3 needed fixes (saas-multi-tenancy status echoes, durable-sagas LIFO/SCREAMING_SNAKE, platform-agent AF-4/Super-judge)
retained_by_design: 5 T-numbers in DesignSystemGovernancePage taskTypeId values — RUN-131 intentional 'human label (T617)' format for platform-admin operator cross-reference on compatibility reports

16 CF-numbers as ruleId/ruleA/ruleB/ironRule/policyRule field values on platform-admin BFA + ASM + MetaArbitration pages — stable operator cross-references analogous to Jira ticket IDs in Jira's own UI

9 coverage_not_yet_examined matches in detailed per-cell entries (e.g. 'tenant-admin cell passed, platform-support cell still pending') — these are per-(flow×role×lang×state) cells, not whole-flow gaps, and represent the residual Tier-2 audit work
rounds.notes: Round 3 pulled off the three-punch plan: (1) RUN-139 flipped every text-left/right + ml-auto to logical properties (29+1 saved 30 pts), (2) RUN-140 fixed T-number body copy (14 saved), (3) RUN-141+142 closed the 9 NOT_YET_EXAMINED whole-flow gaps and updated the matrix index (8 saved). Remaining 51 pts: 10 from retained T-IDs, 32 from platform-admin CF rule IDs, 9 from per-cell coverage gaps. These are all 'retained by design' or 'detailed-cell' items that no longer move under bulk patterns.

rounds.label: Round 4 — convergence check + residual qualitative pass
r4_qualitative_work_committed: 23 AdminCrudPanel descriptions: 'FLOW-NN admin console backed by /api/dynamic/xiigen-*' → 'Raw index browser (admin debug) — reads /api/dynamic/xiigen-*'
AiSelfModificationPage: 3 body-text FLOW-29 + FLOW-44 references rewritten to human labels ('deep-research flow', subtitle FLOW-44 prefix stripped)
DynamicFormsWorkflowsPage: subtitle rewrote 'FLOW-21 · Form schema DSL' → plain-language explanation; tenant-admin tip dropped FLOW-23 inline reference
rounds.why_score_did_not_move: The v2 score targets acronym / AI-slop / physical-direction leaks, all of which are at 0 user-visible or retained-by-design. R4 fixes improve qualitative UX (Nielsen H2 match-real-world) but the changes fall below the current weight set's threshold — there is no weight yet for 'FLOW-NN in user-visible subtitle'. A future weight-refresh could add that axis, but the cell-level audit (ROUND-2-COVERAGE-MATRIX.json) is the right home for those observations, not the automated score.

residual_51pt_floor.description: 51 points are the architectural floor: retained-by-design platform-admin identifiers + per-cell coverage gaps. None will move under bulk automated patterns.

breakdown.10_pts: 5 T-IDs × weight 2 — DesignSystemGovernance compatibility-report taskTypeId suffixes (RUN-131 intentional)
breakdown.32_pts: 16 CF-IDs × weight 2 — platform-admin rule-catalog IDs on BFA / ASM / MetaArbitration engines (Jira-in-Jira pattern)
breakdown.9_pts: 9 NOT_YET_EXAMINED × weight 1 — per-cell gaps in the detailed matrix (e.g. 'tenant-admin passed, platform-support cell pending') — these are Tier-2 cell audits, not whole-flow misses

instructions_for_future_sessions.record_after_round_end: add a new 'round' entry with commit hash + offences_v2_user_visible + score_v2 + delta_vs_prev + improvement_pct
instructions_for_future_sessions.measuring_historical_commits: Copy scripts/ux-quality-score.sh to /tmp/ (outside worktree) so it survives checkouts. Stash current changes. Run: git checkout <sha> -- client/src/pages docs/screen-examination && bash /tmp/ux-quality-score.sh && git checkout HEAD -- client/src/pages docs/screen-examination. Repeat for each historical SHA. Finally git stash pop to restore.
instructions_for_future_sessions.convergence_check: if last round improvement_pct < 1, declare converged and stop — remaining work is per-cell / architectural

known_limits_of_this_measurement.automatable_axes_only: The score measures axes that can be grep-counted: plain-language acronyms, AI-slop patterns, physical-direction classes. Nielsen H1/H2/H8/H9 scoring, interface-design mandate checks (swap/squint/signature), role-branching correctness, business-logic-state fidelity — these still require visual review and are tracked cell-by-cell in ROUND-2-COVERAGE-MATRIX.json, not by this score.
known_limits_of_this_measurement.score_can_plateau_while_quality_still_lo: If all grep-countable offences reach 0 but 30 flows are still NOT_YET_EXAMINED in the coverage matrix, the score understates residual work. The convergence criterion should be 'score-delta < 1% AND coverage_NOT_YET_EXAMINED = 0' — otherwise we are just converging on the automatable subset.
known_limits_of_this_measurement.weights_are_rough: P0 leaks weight 3, P1 weight 2, P2 weight 1. These are heuristic — tuned to reflect Luba's critique priorities. A weight refresh is fine if a new critique category emerges.
